skill.name: flutter-animations
skill.description: Guides Flutter developers through implicit, explicit, hero, staggered, curved, and physics-based animations with practical Dart patterns and reusable examples.
skill.summary: Create smooth Flutter animations with the right widget, controller, curve, and performance pattern.
skill.author: madteacher

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static alerts are false positives caused by Flutter and Markdown terminology. The certificate/key hits are Dart constructor parameters such as super.key, weak-crypto hits are animation classes such as SpringDescription, and external-command hits are Markdown code spans or Dart examples. No prompt injection, shell execution, credential handling, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Flutter key Parameters
low_findings.description: The high certificate/key detections refer to Flutter widget constructor parameters such as super.key. They are not certificate files, private keys, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use Flutter Key plumbing in widget constructors. This is normal Dart UI code and contains no secret values or key files.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Code Formatting
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown backticks used to format Dart symbols and fenced Dart examples. They do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation and Dart code blocks. No Ruby, shell, child process, or command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Animation Physics Names
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match Flutter animation physics names such as SpringDescription. These are motion simulation APIs, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is Flutter spring animation configuration with mass, stiffness, and damping. It has no hashing, encryption, signing, or credential use.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Benign Flutter Examples
low_findings.description: Reconnaissance-related detections map to Flutter UI examples, image loading examples, and documentation text. No system discovery or network probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are ordinary Flutter sample code, including app startup, Image.network usage, and platform-specific spring tuning. They do not inspect the host environment or scan networks.

content.value_statement: Flutter animation APIs can be hard to choose and combine correctly. This skill provides practical guidance for selecting patterns, writing Dart animation code, and improving motion performance.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use implicit, explicit, hero, staggered, and physics-based animations.
Provides Dart patterns for AnimationController, Tween, AnimatedWidget, and AnimatedBuilder.
Guides shared element transitions with Hero widgets and custom flight behavior.
Shows staggered timing with intervals, multiple tweens, and coordinated controllers.
Recommends curves, durations, disposal patterns, and performance checks for Flutter UI motion.
limitations: Does not run or profile a Flutter app by itself.
Does not replace device testing for animation smoothness and accessibility.
Example code may need adaptation for project architecture and state management.
Focuses on Flutter framework animations, not game engines or custom rendering pipelines.

use_cases.title: Add Simple State Animations
use_cases.description: Choose implicit widgets for fades, size changes, padding changes, and simple visual feedback.

use_cases.title: Design Shared Element Navigation
use_cases.description: Plan Hero tags, image transitions, route behavior, and custom flight paths between screens.

use_cases.title: Tune Complex Motion Systems
use_cases.description: Coordinate staggered, curved, and physics-based animations for polished production interfaces.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Animation Type
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to animate [property or interaction] in a Flutter screen. Recommend the best animation type and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Implicit Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement a Flutter implicit animation for [widget behavior]. Include duration, curve, and state update guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Explicit Controller Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an explicit Flutter animation using AnimationController for [effect]. Include lifecycle, Tween setup, and disposal details.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Complex Animation Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Flutter animation plan for [feature]. Identify controller structure, stagger timing, Hero usage, curves, and performance risks.

output_examples.input: Request: Add a smooth expand and fade effect to a Flutter card.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends an implicit animation when the effect follows a state change. It suggests AnimatedContainer for size, AnimatedOpacity for visibility, a short easeInOut curve, and an onEnd callback only if follow-up work is needed.

output_examples.input: Request: Create a product image transition from a grid to a detail page.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes a Hero transition with stable unique tags, matching source and destination widgets, image fit guidance, and optional custom flight behavior for shape or aspect ratio changes.

output_examples.input: Request: Make a list reveal one item at a time after loading completes.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests a staggered animation with one controller, interval-based opacity and slide tweens, and testing guidance for frame timing on target devices.

best_practices: Use implicit animations for simple state changes before adding controllers.
Dispose every AnimationController and keep rebuild scopes as small as practical.
Test motion on target devices and honor reduced-motion accessibility needs.
anti_patterns: Do not use multiple controllers when one interval-based controller is enough.
Do not animate expensive layout work when a transform can create the same effect.
Do not add decorative motion without a clear user interaction or feedback purpose.

faq.question: When should I use implicit animations?
faq.answer: Use them for simple property changes triggered by state, such as opacity, padding, size, alignment, or color.

faq.question: When do I need AnimationController?
faq.answer: Use AnimationController when you need lifecycle control, repeat behavior, pause behavior, status listeners, or coordinated multiple values.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Hero animations?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Hero tags, source and destination widgets, shared element transitions, and custom flight behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Flutter apps?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and example structures, but you must adapt them to your app routes, state, assets, and design system.

faq.question: How does it address performance?
faq.answer: It recommends smaller rebuild scopes, controller disposal, appropriate transforms, curve choices, and profiling with Flutter tools.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance works for AI-assisted Flutter implementation, review, and refactoring in those tools.
